title: Investing $1,000 in These 3 Dividend Stocks Would Be a Smart Move This May
text: Embedded rent growth should drive Prologis' dividend higher for the next several years. Crown Castle profits by leasing must-have cell tower space, and shares those profits with its investors.

title: Is A Commercial Real Estate Crisis Coming? These Experts Say No
text: People seem to think that commercial real estate is crashing. The truth is the opposite. Lower quality Class B and C office buildings are indeed suffering mightily right now, while Class A office is mainly suffering from negative sentiment.

title: ETFs in Focus as Banking Crisis Scars U.S. Real Estate Sector
text: Being the only sector to lag behind the S&P 500, the U.S. real estate market is expected to remain under pressure this year. Against the backdrop of rising interest rates and tightening credit policies of the bank, take a look into some ETFs that can help.

title: Slowing Demand & Economic Woes: Are Industrial REITs at Risk?
text: While decelerating consumer demand and economic uncertainty pose challenges, industrial REITs like PLD, REXR, COLD and EGP exhibit resilience through strong occupancy and healthy rates.

title: This Top Dividend Stock Showcases Why It's a Screaming Buy
text: Prologis posted exceptionally strong first-quarter numbers. Demand for warehouse space is resilient, while supplies are still very tight.

title: What Did These CEOs Of Companies With Crashing Stocks Get? A Raise
text: Investors took a big financial shellacking last year, with the S&P 500 falling nearly 20%. But many CEOs of the companies with shares dropping the most still got a raise.

title: Prologis says ‘market is going to tighten up again'
text: Management from logistics real estate giant Prologis said Tuesday the nation's warehouse market will remain tight moving forward as the supply side is facing headwinds. The company said “true months of supply” — a measure of current vacancies and space coming online compared to trailing net absorption — stands at 30 months.

title: Prologis (PLD) Beats Q1 FFO Estimates on Solid Rent Growth
text: Prologis (PLD) puts up a decent show in the first quarter on robust activity and rent growth.

title: Prologis (PLD) Q1 FFO and Revenues Beat Estimates
text: Prologis (PLD) came out with quarterly funds from operations (FFO) of $1.22 per share, beating the Zacks Consensus Estimate of $1.21 per share. This compares to FFO of $1.09 per share a year ago.

title: Prologis tops revenue expectations, lifts FFO outlook as ‘demand remains healthy'
text: Prologis Inc. PLD, +2.82% reported Tuesday first-quarter revenue that rose above expectations, as funds from operations (FFO) that matched forecasts, saying demand remains “healthy.” The logistics real estate company's stock slipped 0.1% in premarket trading.

title: Key Factors to Impact Prologis (PLD) This Earnings Season
text: Prologis' (PLD) Q1 results are likely to reflect the benefits of its premium facilities at strategic locations and expansion efforts, though moderation in demand might partly limit the growth tempo.

title: Billionaire Investor Says 'Buy REITs'
text: Billionaire Barry Sternlicht is buying REITs. He explains that there are "unbelievable bargains" in REITs.

title: The Big Myth About Industrial REITs
text: Stories are suggesting that we're finally seeing a downturn in industrial real estate demand. They're based on weak evidence. Occupancy and leasing spreads remain high. Guidance still calls for strong rent growth to continue. This is more relevant than a blip in the number of people estimated to work.

title: What I Wish I Knew Before Investing In REITs

title: The Market Timer's Hall Of Fame Is An Empty Room

title: 3 Industrial REIT Stocks to Buy in April
text: Prologis, Rexford, and Terreno are very different sizes but have similar performance. Each of these REITs has its niche, too, and has outperformed the overall market for years.

title: 5 REITs I Felt Like Writing About
text: Strong demand from consumers pushes tenants to continue leasing space. The need for space drives revenues, which drives AFFO per share. Growth rates are lower in some cases for 2023 because of variable rate debt.

title: 17 Fast-Growing REITs With 17 Strong Balance Sheets
text: A FROG is a REIT that shows a Fast Rate of Growth and a strong balance sheet. This article discusses how to identify a FROG, and lists all the FROGs as we head deeper into 2023.

title: 3 Top Stocks to Buy in April
text: Brookfield Infrastructure expects to grow its earnings by 10% this year. Prologis is on track to expand its earnings by 9.5% in 2023 despite its recessionary outlook.

title: 3 Premium Dividend Stocks I'd Buy Without Hesitation This April
text: Brookfield Infrastructure has increased its payout for 14 straight years. Enterprise Products Partners notched its 24th consecutive year of increasing its payout in 2022.
